public_api_summary: Job scheduling library with fluent API: schedule.every(N).unit.do(func). Supports seconds, minutes, hours, days, weeks with .at() times and timezone support.
description: Python job scheduling for humans. An in-process scheduler for periodic jobs that uses a simple, human-friendly syntax.
risks: Single-file module with 945 lines - large refactoring could be complex
Timezone handling uses pytz which has edge cases around DST transitions
No async support - blocking calls during job execution
In-process only - no distributed scheduling capabilities
